itineraries.name: The Classic Sherry Triangle: 3 Days
itineraries.theme: A comprehensive tour of all three Triangle towns: Jerez de la Frontera, El Puerto de Santa Maria and Sanlucar de Barrameda

days.morning: Guided tour of Gonzalez Byass: see the historic solera cellars, the cooperage and the Tio Pepe fino veil. Book through the Gonzalez Byass website.
days.lunch: Bar Juanito, Calle Pescaderia Vieja 8, Jerez: the classic sherry-tapas bar where fino is poured at the bar alongside tortillitas de camarones (prawn fritters).
days.afternoon: Visit Bodega Tradicion on Plaza Cordobeses: private tasting of VORS Amontillado and Oloroso in the Spanish Golden Age art room.
days.evening: Walk the Casco Antiguo and visit Tabanco El Pasaje on Calle Santa Maria for flamenco cantaores and a copa of fino beside the barrels.
days.dinner: La Carbona, Calle San Francisco de Paula 2, Jerez: sherry-focused tasting menu in a converted bodega.
days.accommodation: Hotel Bellas Artes, Plaza del Arroyo 45, Jerez: boutique hotel in a converted 18th-century townhouse.

days.morning: Visit Osborne bodega, Calle Los Moros 7: tour the historic cellars including the famous bull paintings collection. Book via Osborne website.
days.lunch: Ribera del Marisco seafood street on the El Puerto waterfront: grilled coquinas (tiny clams) and langostinos with Fino Quinta.
days.afternoon: Visit Aponiente for a cellar tour (if not dining): the restored tidal mill in the Guadalete salt marshes is an architectural landmark.
days.evening: Take the catamaran ferry across the Bay of Cadiz to the old city for an aperitivo hour on the Atlantic.
days.dinner: Casa Flores, Calle Ribera del Rio 9, El Puerto: traditional Cadiz seafood with a strong fino list.
days.accommodation: Hotel Monasterio de San Miguel, Calle Larga 27, El Puerto de Santa Maria: converted 18th-century convent.

days.morning: Walk the Barrio Alto bodega circuit: visit Barbadillo (Calle Sevilla 1) and Hidalgo La Gitana (Banda Playa 42) for back-to-back manzanilla tastings.
days.lunch: Casa Balbino, Plaza del Cabildo 14, Sanlucar: the definitive langostino experience with chilled Manzanilla under the town square arcades.
days.afternoon: Take the Donana boat tour from the Bajo de Guia quayside for 90 minutes in the wetland reserve.
days.evening: Return to the Plaza del Cabildo and choose any tabanco for a final copa of en rama Manzanilla before the drive home.
days.dinner: Mirador de Doñana, Bajo de Guia, Sanlucar: grilled Atlantic fish on the Guadalquivir bank.
days.accommodation: Los Helechos, Plaza Madre de Dios 9, Sanlucar de Barrameda: charming courtyard hotel in the old town.

itineraries.tip: The Triangle can be done in a circuit; base yourself in Jerez or El Puerto and do day trips to Sanlucar. All three towns are within 30km of each other.

verified.address_quoted: Jerez de la Frontera, El Puerto de Santa Maria, Sanlucar de Barrameda

itineraries.name: Albariza Single-Vineyard Pilgrimage: 2 Days
itineraries.theme: The new-wave pago producers: Cota 45, Forlong, Luis Perez and the single-vineyard Palomino revolution

days.morning: Visit Bodegas Cota 45 by prior appointment: Ramiro Ibanez leads a walk through the named albariza pagos (Balbainas, Balbaina Alta) and pours the UBE range of unfortified Palomino whites.
days.lunch: Casa Balbino, Plaza del Cabildo 14, Sanlucar: langostinos and manzanilla.
days.afternoon: Drive to the Pago del Humo outside El Puerto de Santa Maria: visit Forlong by prior appointment. Rocio Ros and Alberto Orte show the organic albariza vineyard and pour the biodynamic-practice unfortified Palomino and fortified ranges.
days.evening: Return to Sanlucar for a tabanco evening at Tabanco Las Banderillas.
days.dinner: Mirador de Doñana, Bajo de Guia, Sanlucar: grilled Atlantic fish on the river.
days.accommodation: Los Helechos, Plaza Madre de Dios 9, Sanlucar de Barrameda.

days.morning: Visit Bodegas Luis Perez at Carretera Jerez-Medina Sidonia km 2.3: Luis Perez leads a tour of his organic vineyards and pours the Pandemia and Garrotin unfortified wines alongside his traditional fortified range.
days.lunch: Bar Juanito, Calle Pescaderia Vieja 8, Jerez: tortillitas de camarones and fino.
days.afternoon: Visit Bodega Tradicion on Plaza Cordobeses 3 for VORS expressions showing how ancient pago wines express themselves after decades in solera.
days.evening: Tabanco Plateros, Calle Plateros 38, Jerez: barrel-drawn fino in the city's most traditional tabanco.
days.dinner: La Carbona, Calle San Francisco de Paula 2, Jerez.
days.accommodation: Hotel Bellas Artes, Plaza del Arroyo 45, Jerez de la Frontera.

itineraries.tip: Both Cota 45 and Forlong require advance booking and are not walk-in tourist bodegas; contact through their websites several weeks ahead.

verified.address_quoted: Sanlucar de Barrameda, El Puerto de Santa Maria, Jerez de la Frontera

itineraries.name: Manzanilla Weekend in Sanlucar and Donana: 2 Days
itineraries.theme: An immersive Atlantic Manzanilla and nature weekend based entirely in Sanlucar de Barrameda

days.morning: Barbadillo bodega tour (Calle Sevilla 1): the most comprehensive public tour in Sanlucar, covering the production process from Palomino harvest through solera maturation to en rama bottling.
days.lunch: Casa Balbino, Plaza del Cabildo 14: the quintessential Sanlucar lunch. Langostinos, chicharrones and manzanilla.
days.afternoon: Hidalgo La Gitana bodega visit (Banda Playa 42): compare La Gitana with the La Gitana Pasada en Rama to experience biological ageing across different maturation stages.
days.evening: Tabanco El Guitarron de San Pedro: a small tabanco on the Barrio Bajo with flamenco on Friday evenings.
days.dinner: Restaurante Bigote, Bajo de Guia, Sanlucar: the classic Sanlucar seafood institution on the Guadalquivir bank.
days.accommodation: Posada de Palacio, Calle Caballeros 11, Sanlucar de Barrameda: a boutique palace hotel in the historic Barrio Alto.

days.morning: Early morning Donana boat tour from Bajo de Guia quayside: 90-minute guided tour through Europe's largest wetland reserve.
days.lunch: Picnic on the Donana return: Barbadillo provides a manzanilla-and-tapas picnic option for boat tour groups. Alternatively, return to Bajo de Guia for grilled fish.
days.afternoon: Argueso bodega (Calle Mar 8): tasting of the San Leon Manzanilla Reserva (manzanilla pasada) to close the Sanlucar tasting arc.
days.evening: Final sunset copita on the Calzada de la Playa beachfront with chilled manzanilla watching the Guadalquivir mouth.
days.dinner: Taberna Argos, Calle Ancha 13, Sanlucar: local seafood and manzanilla.
days.accommodation: Posada de Palacio, Calle Caballeros 11, Sanlucar de Barrameda.

itineraries.tip: August visitors can combine this itinerary with the Carreras de Caballos en la Playa beach horse races for the most atmospheric Manzanilla experience of the year.

itineraries.name: Andalucia Grand Wine Tour: 4 Days
itineraries.theme: Sherry Triangle combined with Montilla-Moriles PX country and Ronda mountain wines

days.morning: Gonzalez Byass tour and Tio Pepe fino tasting.
days.afternoon: Emilio Lustau bodega tour (Plaza del Cubo 4): the Almacenista range and en rama fino.
days.evening: Tabanco El Pasaje, Calle Santa Maria, Jerez: flamenco cantaores beside the barrels.
days.accommodation: Hotel Bellas Artes, Plaza del Arroyo 45, Jerez.

days.location: Sanlucar de Barrameda and El Puerto de Santa Maria
days.morning: Barbadillo tour in Sanlucar; en rama manzanilla tasting.
days.lunch: Casa Balbino, Plaza del Cabildo 14, Sanlucar.
days.afternoon: Osborne bodega tour in El Puerto de Santa Maria.
days.evening: Waterfront aperitivo on El Puerto's Ribera del Marisco.
days.dinner: Seafood dinner at El Puerto waterfront.
days.accommodation: Hotel Monasterio de San Miguel, El Puerto de Santa Maria.

days.morning: Drive north via A-4/A-45 to Montilla (c. 2 hours). Bodegas Alvear tour: the Pedro Ximenez asoleo platform and historic soleras.
days.lunch: Taberna Sociedad de Plateros, Calle San Francisco 6, Cordoba.
days.afternoon: Cordoba Mezquita visit; Bodegas Perez Barquero in Montilla for comparison tasting of Fino de Montilla versus Jerez fino.
days.evening: Return to Jerez or overnight in Cordoba.
days.dinner: Bodegas Campos, Calle Los Lineros 32, Cordoba: classic Cordoba bodega-restaurant.
days.accommodation: Hotel Amistad Cordoba, Plaza de Maimonides, Cordoba.

days.morning: Drive to Ronda via A-382 (c. 1.5 hours from Jerez). Bodegas F. Schatz visit: organic estate at 700 metres altitude.
days.lunch: Almocabar, Calle Ruedo Alameda 5, Ronda: local Serrania cuisine.
days.afternoon: Puente Nuevo viewpoint and Ronda bullring; Bodegas Descalzos Viejos wine tasting.
days.evening: Return to Jerez via the scenic A-375 road through the Cadiz hinterland.
days.dinner: La Carbona, Calle San Francisco de Paula 2, Jerez: final sherry-pairing dinner.
days.accommodation: Hotel Bellas Artes, Plaza del Arroyo 45, Jerez.

itineraries.tip: Day 3 is long; consider splitting it by overnight in Cordoba for a more relaxed pace.

verified.address_quoted: Jerez de la Frontera, Sanlucar de Barrameda, Cordoba, Ronda

itineraries.name: Vendimia Harvest Week in Jerez: 5 Days
itineraries.theme: The harvest season in early September: Fiesta de la Vendimia, asoleo process visits, and new-vintage first tastings

days.morning: Arrive and check in; walk the albariza vineyards on the Jerez Superior plateau before harvest.
days.afternoon: Gonzalez Byass pre-harvest cellar visit: see the harvest preparation in the sorting area.
days.evening: Tabanco Plateros for the first copa of fino.
days.dinner: La Carbona for a sherry-pairing dinner.
days.accommodation: Hotel Bellas Artes, Plaza del Arroyo 45.

days.morning: Fiesta de la Vendimia opening ceremony at the Collegiate Church of San Salvador on the first Sunday of September: grape treading, blessing and first venencia ceremony.
days.lunch: Post-ceremony public tasting zone in the Plaza de Asuncion.
days.afternoon: Visit Emilio Lustau bodega (Plaza del Cubo 4): see the first must of the season being classified for fino versus oloroso destiny.
days.evening: Peña Flamenca buleria singing in the old city.
days.dinner: El Gallo Azul, Calle Larga 2, Jerez: historic casino building turned restaurant.

days.morning: Barbadillo harvest visit: the asoleo PX sun-drying mats laid out beside the bodega.
days.lunch: Casa Balbino, Plaza del Cabildo 14, Sanlucar.
days.afternoon: Bodegas Cota 45 visit by appointment: Ramiro Ibanez discussing the harvest character of specific albariza pagos.
days.evening: Tabanco Las Banderillas for en rama manzanilla.
days.accommodation: Posada de Palacio, Calle Caballeros 11, Sanlucar.

days.morning: Osborne harvest visit (Calle Los Moros 7): the Osborne harvest festival events include public vineyard walks.
days.afternoon: Catamaran to Cadiz for an afternoon in the oldest wine-trading port in western Europe.
days.evening: Return by catamaran and aperitivo at an El Puerto tabanco.
days.dinner: Casa Flores, Calle Ribera del Rio 9, El Puerto.
days.accommodation: Hotel Monasterio de San Miguel, El Puerto.

days.morning: Visit Bodega Tradicion (Plaza Cordobeses 3) for final VORS tasting: context for how this year's harvest wine will eventually taste after 30 years in solera.
days.afternoon: Drive or transfer to Jerez airport or Seville for connections.

itineraries.tip: The Fiesta de la Vendimia date changes year to year (first Sunday of September); confirm the exact date via the Consejo Regulador website before booking.
